story.description: The interviewee discusses their family background and upbringing in a predominantly African-American community in Bloomfield. They reflect on issues of racial dynamics, integration, and the evolution of the community over time, highlighting the persistence of discrimination and personal identity amidst societal changes. They share experiences with local politics, expressing a preference for conservative economic policies, and detail their community involvement. The conversation touches on challenges within the education system and housing conditions, emphasizing the need for greater integration and support for struggling students. Despite challenges, Bloomfield is characterized as an evolving, predominantly African-American community with potential for upward mobility.
story.title: Bob Ikes oral history.WAV
